findings.evidence: `rg` hits: line 299 defines `NonnegMassOn Ω c gamma`; line 330 proves `norm_bound`; line 353 proves only `LowerOrderBoundedOn Ω (fun _ => 0) c 0 gamma`, while line 204 shows `LowerOrderBoundedOn` is just `DriftBoundedOn Ω b beta ∧ MassBoundedOn Ω c gamma`.
findings.fix: Prove the general bridge first, e.g. `NonnegMassOn.mass_boundedOn : MassBoundedOn Ω c gamma` and `NonnegMassOn.lowerOrderBoundedOn (h : NonnegMassOn Ω c gamma) (hb : DriftBoundedOn Ω b beta) : LowerOrderBoundedOn Ω b c beta gamma`, then derive the zero-drift lemma as a special case if desired.
findings.issue: `NonnegMassOn.lowerOrderBoundedOn_zero_drift` fixes the drift coefficient to zero even though `NonnegMassOn` only strengthens the mass hypothesis and should compose with any available drift bound.

summary: The separate drift and mass APIs now sit at the expected pointwise explicit-constant level, but one bridge lemma from nonnegative mass is unnecessarily specialized to zero drift.
